title: 1.1: Tests that execution segment sequence creation returns ordered segment ranges when provided with a valid fractional string sequence.
1.2: Tests that striped offset computation returns deterministic start index offset slice values when applied to an execution segment tuple.
1.3: Tests that execution segment sequence returns a numeric least common denominator when sequence values contain fractional components.
1.4: Tests that execution tuple creation returns a transformed execution sequence when applying a numeric value to an existing execution segment setup.
2.1: Places the test for execution segment string sequence parsing in lib/execution_segment_test.go
2.2: Places the test for striped offset computation from execution tuples in lib/execution_segment_test.go.
2.3: Places the test for numeric property derivation from execution segment sequences in lib/execution_segment_test.go.
2.4: Places the test for execution tuple creation from numeric value input in lib/execution_segment_test.go.
3.1: All tests use the Go testing framework with assertion helpers from the required package to gate failure conditions.
3.2: All tests follow the Go test naming convention using exported functions prefixed with Test to enable automatic discovery.
3.3: All tests perform input construction followed by execution segment operation invocation followed by result verification through assertions.
4.1: Follows table-driven input definition for the test case validating execution segment sequence creation.
4.2: Follows table-driven input definition for the test case validating striped offset computation behavior.
4.3: Follows table-driven input definition for the test case validating numeric property derivation from execution segment sequences.
4.4: Follows table-driven input definition for the test case validating execution tuple creation from numeric value input.
